title: Non-record: Looped Transformer + LoRA + Skip Connections + NorMuon + SWA + Int6 + Sliding Window
body:
## Summary

**Looped depth recurrence + per-virtual-layer LoRA + encoder-decoder skip connections** — a novel architecture that maximizes model depth per stored byte, combined with the best proven training and quantization techniques.

### Architecture
- **5 unique transformer blocks** looped to **30 virtual layers** (6x depth multiplier, only 5 blocks stored)
- **Encoder-decoder skip connections**: 15 encoder layers store skip tensors, 15 decoder layers consume them in reverse via learned `skip_weights`
- **Per-virtual-layer LoRA adapters** (rank=4) on Q,V projections differentiate each virtual layer at ~1.5% parameter overhead
- **Residual mixing** (`resid_mix`): learned blend of hidden state with original embedding at each layer

### Training
- **NorMuon optimizer** (per-row normalized Newton-Schulz orthogonalization)
- **Wallclock-aware warmdown** (LR decay triggers on remaining wall time, not step count)
- **Stochastic Weight Averaging** (7 checkpoint average during warmdown)
- **Gradient clipping** (norm=1.0) for stability through 30 virtual layers
- **Tuned LRs**: MATRIX_LR=0.02, MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000
- **seq_len=4096** training for richer context

### Export & Eval
- **Int6 quantization** with fp16 embedding + LoRA passthrough (~14.9 MB artifact)
- **Sliding window evaluation** (seq=4096, stride=64) for ~0.035 BPB free improvement

### Key Insight

Naive weight sharing (PR #31: 1.2663 BPB, *worse* than baseline) fails because identical layers cannot specialize. Per-virtual-layer LoRA + skip connections solve this: 30 pairs of rank-4 adapters add only ~307K params but enable each virtual layer to develop distinct attention patterns, while skip connections provide gradient highways through the deep virtual network.

### Results (1xH100, 203M tokens — prior version)

| Run | Pre-quant BPB | Post-quant BPB | Tokens |
|-----|--------------|----------------|--------|
| Run 3 (int4, no skips) | 1.50 | 1.60 | 203M |

### Estimated improvements (this version)

| Technique | Est. Impact |
|-----------|------------|
| Int6 quant + fp16 embed | -0.082 |
| Sliding window eval | -0.035 |
| Skip connections + resid_mix | -0.02 to -0.03 |
| NorMuon + tuned LRs + warmdown fix | -0.015 to -0.025 |
| SWA | -0.003 to -0.005 |
| **Estimated 1xH100 BPB** | **~1.35-1.40** |

### 8xH100 Projection

With ~1.77B tokens (8.7x more data, ~3.1 doublings at -0.09/doubling):
- **Projected: ~1.07-1.12 BPB** (would beat current SOTA ~1.163)

Pending compute grant for 8xH100 validation.

## Test plan

- [x] Syntax verified
- [x] 3 successful 1xH100 runs (prior version)
- [x] Artifact size estimated ~14.9 MB (under 16 MB cap)
- [ ] 8xH100 official run (pending compute)
- [ ] Verify int6 roundtrip BPB matches estimates

comments.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Add optimizer for untied lm_head weights**

When `TIE_EMBEDDINGS=0`, `LoopedGPT` uses `lm_head` for logits, but the optimizer list only includes token embeddings, block params, scalars, and LoRA; `lm_head.weight` is never stepped. In that configuration the output head stays at initialization, so training cannot meaningfully converge despite `HEAD_LR` being exposed as a hyperparameter.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
